title: American Built Cabinets Supports the BIABA Golf Tournament
description: The American Built Cabinets team volunteered at this year's BIABA Golf Tournament, reconnecting with Bay Area builders, designers, developers, contractors, and suppliers.
content: The American Built Cabinets team was honored to volunteer at this year's BIABA Golf Tournament. The event was a great opportunity to reconnect with industry partners and spend time with the builders, designers, developers, contractors, and suppliers who help shape communities across the Bay Area. As a local cabinet manufacturer, we believe successful projects are built on more than quality craftsmanship. They begin with strong relationships, open communication, and a shared commitment to building better homes and communities. We are grateful to everyone who helped make this year's tournament a success and appreciate the opportunity to support the organizations and professionals who continue to strengthen the Bay Area building industry. Thank you to BIABA and everyone who participated. We look forward to continuing to build lasting partnerships, both on and off the jobsite. Built Stronger. Built Smarter. Built Local.

title: American Built Cabinets Proudly Sponsored the KTSF 50th Anniversary Summer Concert
description: American Built Cabinets sponsored the KTSF 50th Anniversary Summer Concert in Brentwood, supporting community partnerships and cultural events throughout the Bay Area.
content: American Built Cabinets was proud to sponsor the KTSF 50th Anniversary Summer Concert , joining families, local businesses, community leaders, and supporters from across the Bay Area for an evening celebrating music, culture, and community. Held at Hannah Nicole Vineyards in Brentwood, the anniversary event commemorated KTSF ’s 50 years of service to the Chinese-American community. Guests enjoyed live performances, wine tasting, vineyard tours, and special appearances in a welcoming outdoor setting. As a California-based cabinet manufacturer, American Built Cabinets believes that supporting the communities where we live and work is an important part of responsible local manufacturing. Our commitment extends beyond producing quality cabinetry to building lasting relationships with local organizations, businesses, and community partners. We congratulate KTSF on this significant milestone and thank the organizers, performers, volunteers, sponsors, and guests who helped make the celebration possible. American Built Cabinets looks forward to continuing its support of meaningful events and partnerships throughout the Bay Area.

title: American Built Cabinets Celebrates America’s 250th Anniversary
description: American Built Cabinets celebrated America’s 250th Anniversary with a special Fourth of July banner flight across the Bay Area. The celebration reflects our commitment to American manufacturing, local craftsmanship, and the communities we proudly serve.
content: This Fourth of July, American Built Cabinets celebrated America’s 250th Anniversary by taking our message to the skies. Our banner flew across the Bay Area as a tribute to the nation’s milestone and the values that inspire our work every day. As a Bay Area cabinet manufacturer, we believe great craftsmanship starts close to home. Building locally allows us to deliver exceptional quality, support our community, and provide reliable solutions for builders, designers, and homeowners. On this Independence Day, we proudly celebrate the people, partnerships, and spirit of innovation that continue to shape American manufacturing. Built Stronger. Built Smarter. Built Local. Happy Fourth of July from everyone at American Built Cabinets!

title: American Built Cabinets at the GBI Manufacturing Summit
description: American Built Cabinets participated in the GBI Summit to discuss localized manufacturing, standardized supply chains, and the future of cabinet solutions for North American residential construction.
content: According to the recent GBI Summit coverage by Phoenix TV, one of the most significant trends shaping North America’s construction industry is the continued shift toward localized manufacturing and standardized project delivery. During the panel discussion, Home Building Materials Manufacturing and Supply Chain Development in North America , industry leaders agreed that developers and builders are placing greater emphasis on supply chain reliability, manufacturing consistency, and regulatory compliance when selecting project partners. As a U.S.-based cabinet manufacturer, American Built Cabinets was invited to participate in this industry discussion, where representatives shared perspectives on scalable manufacturing, engineering support, and localized production for residential developments across North America. Operating from its Silicon Valley manufacturing facility, American Built Cabinets combines advanced automation with quality management to support both production builders and custom residential projects. The factory is equipped with four German-engineered automated production systems that enable precision manufacturing, consistent quality, and stable production capacity. The company’s cabinetry is certified to KCMA quality standards and complies with CARB P2 environmental requirements, supporting projects that require reliable performance and regulatory compliance. To date, American Built Cabinets has supplied cabinetry solutions for more than 30 multifamily and residential developments throughout North America, serving both traditional construction and modular building projects with integrated design, manufacturing, and installation services. The discussions at the GBI Summit reinforced an industry-wide consensus that localized manufacturing and standardized supply chains will continue to shape the future of North American residential construction. American Built Cabinets remains committed to investing in advanced manufacturing capabilities and strengthening partnerships with developers, builders, and industry professionals to support this evolving market.

title: American Built Cabinets July 4 San Francisco Sky Banner Event
description: Celebrate Independence Day with American Built Cabinets as our branded sky banner flies over San Francisco on July 4. Learn where to watch, how to participate, and how to enter for a chance to win prizes.
content: American Built Cabinets is proud to celebrate Independence Day with a special aerial banner event above the San Francisco waterfront. On July 4 , our branded banner will fly across the city skyline during one of the Bay Area’s most anticipated holiday celebrations, inviting residents and visitors to look up, capture the moment, and take part in an exciting community giveaway. As America commemorates its 250th anniversary, this event reflects our commitment to American manufacturing, local craftsmanship, and the communities we proudly serve throughout Northern California. Based in San Jose, American Built Cabinets has built its reputation on delivering custom cabinetry designed and manufactured in the United States, and this celebration is our way of sharing that pride beyond the showroom. During the evening, spectators gathered along the San Francisco waterfront may see the American Built Cabinets banner flying above popular Independence Day viewing locations. Guests who photograph the banner and share their images on social media will have the opportunity to enter our July 4 giveaway . Selected participants will have the chance to receive gift cards valued at up to $1,000 , along with additional prizes. Complete participation details and official contest rules will be announced through our official social media channels before the event. The flight is scheduled between 7:00 PM and 9:00 PM on July 4, 2026 . Flight times, routes, and visibility may vary depending on weather conditions, FAA regulations, and operational requirements. Whether you are celebrating with family, friends, or fellow Bay Area residents, we invite you to join us in looking toward the San Francisco skyline as we celebrate Independence Day together. We look forward to seeing you on July 4!

title: American Built Cabinets Hosts Successful Rebranding Open House in San Jose
description: American Built Cabinets welcomed designers, builders, architects, and industry professionals to celebrate its rebranding and showcase local manufacturing capabilities at a successful Open House event in San Jose.
content: On June 10, American Built Cabinets proudly hosted its Rebranding Open House at our San Jose showroom, welcoming designers, builders, architects, and industry professionals from throughout the Bay Area. The event marked an important milestone for our company as we officially introduced the American Built Cabinets brand and shared our vision for the future. Guests had the opportunity to explore our newly rebranded showroom, learn more about our local manufacturing capabilities , and connect with fellow professionals in the design and construction community. Throughout the evening, attendees enjoyed networking opportunities, showroom tours, refreshments, and meaningful conversations about the future of residential design, construction, and custom cabinetry in Northern California. We were honored to welcome Jim Gurule , NKBA Northern California Membership Chair , whose participation and support helped make the event especially memorable. At American Built Cabinets, we believe strong partnerships are the foundation of exceptional projects . This event provided an opportunity to strengthen existing relationships, build new connections, and share the craftsmanship, innovation, and service that define our work. We extend our sincere gratitude to everyone who attended and contributed to the success of this event. Your support continues to inspire our growth and commitment to serving homeowners, designers, builders, and architects throughout Northern California. As we continue this exciting new chapter, we look forward to creating new opportunities for collaboration and delivering high-quality cabinetry solutions that combine thoughtful design, precision manufacturing, and lasting value. Thank you for celebrating with us!

title: American Built Cabinet Exhibits at 2026 BIA | Bay Area PASS Cinco de Mayo Trade Show
description: Events like this are a reminder of how much the Bay Area building community thrives on real, in-person connections. Thanks to BIA | Bay Area and PASS for putting on such a fun event, we're already looking forward to the next one.
content: On May 7, 2026, American Built Cabinet joined the BIA | Bay Area PASS Cinco de Mayo Trade Show Fiesta at the San Ramon Marriott, an afternoon of margaritas, music, and meaningful conversations with builders and industry partners from across the Bay Area. It was great to meet so many local builders at our booth, talk shop, and catch up with familiar faces. Events like this are a reminder of how much the Bay Area building community thrives on real, in-person connections. Thanks to BIA | Bay Area and PASS for putting on such a fun event, we're already looking forward to the next one.

title: American Built Cabinet Attends 2026 BIA | Bay Area Installation of Officers as a Major Sponsor
description: ABC Cabinet Attends 2026 BIA | Bay Area Installation of Officers as a Major Sponsor
content: On January 23, 2026, ABC Cabinet proudly attended the BIA | Bay Area Installation of Officers as a Major Sponsor , joining industry leaders, builders, developers, and partners from across the Bay Area at this annual milestone event. The evening recognized the contributions of the outgoing board of directors and welcomed the 2026 leadership team, highlighting the continued collaboration and leadership that drive the residential building industry forward. As a Bay Area-based cabinetry manufacturer, ABC Cabinet values strong relationships within the local building community. Our participation in this event reflects our ongoing commitment to supporting industry organizations, fostering collaboration, and contributing to the long-term growth of the residential construction sector. We appreciate BIA | Bay Area for hosting a meaningful and well-executed event and look forward to continued engagement and partnership in the years ahead.

title: KABI Co-Hosts ASID CAPEN MIXER on November 13, 2025
description: KABI Co-Hosts ASID CAPEN MIXER on November 13, 2025
content: On November 13 , 2025, KABI successfully co-hosted the ASID CAPEN MIXER in collaboration with the American Society of Interior Designers (ASID) California Peninsula Chapter. The event brought together interior designers, design students, and industry partners from across the Bay Area for an evening of networking and professional exchange. Attendees had the opportunity to connect, share ideas, and explore future collaboration within the design community. As a Bay Area-based local factory specializing in custom cabinetry and modern kitchen systems , KABI shared its approach to craftsmanship, design-driven manufacturing, and material innovation.  KABI thanks ASID CAPEN and all participants for contributing to a successful and engaging event. The company looks forward to continued connections and future collaborations with the design community.
